model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_CLEAN_UP () Bool
  true)
reasoning: bc0ffc0e971.c: Known use-after-free bug (// ERROR at L43) — for loop iterates `ch = ch->next` after `delete_eth_table(ch)` frees ch. Alarm at L15 (inside delete_eth_table). PC: ENABLE_FEATURE_CLEAN_UP=T AND !__STRICT_ANSI__ AND !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc. The library macros restrict the PC to a non-standard stdlib.h inclusion state (before stdlib.h is fully included, !_STDLIB_H=T). In real compilation with #include <stdlib.h>, _STDLIB_H=T and the PC is not satisfied. However, the bug exists whenever CLEAN_UP=T (regardless of stdlib.h state). PC is over-constrained — misses the real compilation state where the bug also occurs.
